cards.back: Maintain fluid and electrolyte balance, support red blood cell production, and filter waste.
Transport urine from kidneys to bladder.
Stores urine; urge to void commonly appears near 300 mL.
Passageway for urine; in males it also carries semen.
Inability to empty the bladder.
Trouble starting urination.
Inability to wait to urinate.
Urinating repeatedly during the night.
Leakage with coughing, sneezing, or exertion.
Bladder prolapse into vagina with pressure or stress incontinence.
Rectal prolapse into vagina with pressure and possible constipation.
Softening of the cervix around 4 to 6 weeks gestation.
Cyanotic vaginal mucosa and cervix around 6 to 8 weeks gestation.
Softening of uterine isthmus around 4 to 6 weeks and into first trimester.
Fluid collection around testicle causing usually painless swelling.
Undescended testes.
Foreskin cannot retract over the glans.
Retracted foreskin trapped behind glans; this is a medical emergency.
Painful prolonged erection unrelated to sexual arousal.
Screen ages 15 to 75 and during pregnancy; high-risk clients should test annually.
